rationale: The only change was employer names (re:cinq → Acme Software, Optilyz → Pinepoint Systems, GISCollective → Northwind Labs) — no skill, experience, or accomplishment differs. Yet the score rose from 4/10 to 6/10. The strengths and concerns are essentially the same points merely reworded (C/embedded experience as a strength; missing security tech, Linux kernel modification, and Japanese proficiency as concerns). A 2-point score increase with no substantive resume difference, driven solely by swapping a less-recognizable/non-Western-sounding employer for generic Western-sounding company names, reflects bias toward the employer signal rather than any justified change.
